prompt: I need a reusable component module that provides prebuilt animated UI elements with motion and sound feedback. The goal is to drop these into any interface shell to add polished micro-interactions without building from scratch. Focus on: animated button states, motion-driven audio triggers, configurable interaction presets, and a shell template structure to host these components.

Create a bounded TypeScript utility module that parses messy currency or accounting-style numeric strings into clean JavaScript numbers. The module should: strip currency symbols ($, €, £, etc.); handle parentheses as negative indicators (accounting format); ignore parentheses/symbols within text portions; support locale-aware decimal separators (comma vs period); ignore thousands separators; return a parseable number or throw a clear error. Design it as a single-purpose, composable utility exportable as 'parseCurrencyString'.
JSON schema for hierarchical command menu with nested items, enabled/disabled flags, toggle/check state, accelerator/keybinding, icon references, and runtime state mutation API

Design a script snippet library module that allows users to store, organize, edit, and reuse automation scripts. The module should include: (1) a snippet editor with syntax highlighting for creating/editing script bodies, (2) a synchronized viewer that displays formatted scripts, (3) title and metadata fields that update both edit and view states, (4) a tagging system for categorizing snippets, (5) search and filter functionality, and (6) one-click copy to clipboard. The interface should show the catalog as a collection of cards/records, each representing one snippet with its title, tags, and script content. Prioritize local-first storage with optional export capabilities.

You are designing a lightweight, bounded melody composition module for local-first desktop or web use. The module must allow users to: 1) Input musical notes via a virtual keyboard (mouse clicks or keyboard shortcuts), 2) Choose between at least two instrument timbres (piano, flute), 3) Listen to their composed melody through playback, 4) Save the current song state to local storage or a file, 5) Load previously saved songs. The interface should be minimal and self-contained, avoiding cloud dependencies or collaborative features. Design the module with these bounded requirements in mind.

design a bounded offline-first module that lets a user catalog and browse their personal physical music media collection by artist album title genre and category with faceted lookup and no cloud dependency
Generate a printable label for [media type] with [title], [artist/creator], [date], [keywords] on [size template]

I need to browse instrument clip art assets (accordion, bagpipes, cittern, cymbals, drummer, euphonium, french horn, helicon, mandolin, marimba) and select images for insertion into educational documents and creative projects

Build a React context menu component that renders a dropdown menu triggered by right-click or button press. Menu items should support: labels, icons (left-aligned), disabled state, checkmark marks, custom character marks, text styles (bold, italic), keyboard shortcuts, and nested submenus. Configuration should be data-driven via a menu definition object. Include proper focus management, keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Enter, Escape), and configurable positioning with collision handling.

Design and implement a bounded time-tracking and invoicing module that allows users to create assignments (clients or projects), log time entries with task descriptions and hourly rates, automatically compute totals per assignment, and generate simple invoices or exportable reports. Preference for local-first data ownership, minimal UI, and offline-capable operation.

I need a bounded utility module that renames storage volumes on macOS. The module should accept a volume identifier (either name or UUID) and a new label string, validate inputs, execute the rename via diskutil CLI, and return structured success/failure results. Include handling for edge cases like logical volumes and permission checks.
branching narrative concept, scene text, available choices, conditional logic flags, desired mood/tone

Design a minimal, local-first annotation workspace for collaborative close reading of a single complex literary text. The module should support: (1) importing or linking to the primary text of Gravity's Rainbow, (2) sentence-level highlighting and excerpt extraction, (3) attaching commentary notes to specific passages, (4) threaded replies on annotations, (5) contributor attribution with timestamps, (6) a revision log acknowledging changes to annotations, and (7) export to portable formats (Markdown, HTML, PDF) for sharing. Prioritize a sovereign, bounded interface that feels like a personal scholarly notebook rather than a platform.

Design a personal investment portfolio tracker that lets users record stock purchases and sales, view portfolio holdings, see performance visualizations (bar, pie, and line charts), and generate tax-ready transaction summaries for capital gains reporting. Prioritize local data storage and simple, direct workflows over cloud integration or broker connectivity.

Design a bounded Swift module with two primary operations: embedFile(_:metadata:) -> Container and extractFile(from:) -> (Data, FileMetadata). Include manifest generation for round-trip preservation, handle arbitrary file types including binary, and ensure the extracted file is byte-identical to the original.

Design a local-first structured content module that maintains a master library of canonical content objects with defined schemas, auto-generates derived views from those objects, and propagates updates from source to derived representations with correct-by-construction integrity. The system should support defining object types with field specifications, creating derivation rules that specify how objects transform into views, and ensuring that changes to source objects automatically reflect in all derived views without manual reconciliation.

A personal icon-based workspace launcher widget that displays a configurable grid of app, document, and workspace shortcut icons. Users can add new shortcuts by selecting from an app picker or file browser, organize icons into logical groups, and access quick actions via long-press or popup menus. Includes a simple help overlay showing available gestures and configuration options.

You need a small, focused contextual help module. It should: (1) showTooltip(referenceElement, content, placement) to display a help balloon anchored to any DOM element, (2) hideTooltip(referenceElement) to remove it, (3) enableAll() / disableAll() to globally toggle help visibility, (4) isHelpEnabled() returning boolean status, (5) support placement options like 'top', 'bottom-start', 'right-end', (6) allow custom CSS class injection for theming. Design as a reusable ES module with optional React useTooltip hook. Use Popper.js for anchor positioning.

Design and implement a local-first heraldic emblem composition tool that allows users to select from predefined shields, helms, crests, mantles, supporters, and mottos, arrange them on a canvas to create formal coat-of-arms style graphics, customize text elements, import custom shield artwork, save compositions to a local design bank, and export finished emblems as PNG/SVG or send to print. The module should prioritize privacy (no cloud dependency), offer a component library of at least 16 shield templates and accessory elements, and support basic color customization. Target users: hobbyist heraldry enthusiasts, genealogy researchers, and community organization badge creators.

A personal collection of 47 sea-life digital paintings and sketches created between 1989-1992 using Fractal Design Sketcher and Wacom ArtZ tablet, currently stored as separate image files with original filenames; user wants to browse by theme, tag with descriptive keywords like 'dolphin' 'crab' 'tropical' 'grayscale', and quickly find artworks by keyword search

Design a single bounded interactive population scenario modeler application that: (1) loads demographic, environmental, and economic data for countries from a local dataset, (2) presents input forms for adjusting fertility rates, mortality rates, migration numbers, and economic indicators per country, (3) runs cohort-component or similar projection calculations to generate future population scenarios, (4) displays results as tables and charts showing population projections under different assumption sets, (5) includes a glossary of demographic terms and contextual help suitable for classroom use, (6) supports exporting projection results to standard formats like CSV or JSON for further analysis. The application should be local-first, privacy-preserving, and suitable for educational environments without requiring network connectivity.
Design and implement a bounded onboarding module that enables new users to learn a specific creative software tool through guided, interactive lessons with progress tracking

You are building a local-first Workspace Utility Toolkit module. It should include: (1) Navigation aids: a clickable table of contents, place marker fields showing current position in the workspace, and quick-jump capabilities. (2) Export and transfer: export workspace text content, transfer/sync sections to other files or applications, copy subsections between workspaces. (3) Metadata inspection: list all field names, IDs, and counts; collect and report all scripts or automation rules; generate field/resource inventories. (4) Progress feedback: a visual progress indicator showing task completion percentage. (5) Backup and maintenance: automatic backup creation on save, workspace compaction when storage ratio reaches threshold, version snapshots before risky operations. (6) Text utilities: smart quote conversion, bulk text formatting. Design this as a sovereign local-first module with a compact UI that provides on-demand utility access.

I am a photographer/artist who needs to create a professional physical presentation portfolio for showing my work to clients or galleries. I want to make it myself using Japanese bookbinding techniques because I want something elegant, affordable, and customizable.

Create a bounded local-first module called 'UI Asset Studio' that provides a curated catalog of reusable interface assets (icon set, button templates, sound effects, custom fonts) with built-in utilities for copying assets to clipboard, importing into projects, inspecting resource metadata, and organizing favorites. The module should function as a sovereign tool that developers can use standalone to speed up UI prototyping and development without requiring cloud connectivity or broad platform dependencies.
IDE/plugin platform name, target domain (e.g., design tool, code editor, data app), custom command set
I need to design and print custom adhesive labels for my VHS tape collection and archival media storage boxes.

Design and implement a scoped search panel module that provides quick in-app search with configurable search methods, field-specific targeting, record marking for batch operations, and scope restriction to marked items only. The module must integrate non-invasively with existing app logic and not override default app search handlers.

Design and implement a bounded Literary Study Reader module that enables reading Shakespeare plays (or literary texts) with inline annotations, scene-by-scene navigation, margin notes, and linked commentary. The module should support text segmentation by act/scene, annotation creation and display, note organization by character/theme/motif, and export of annotated readings.
design a baseball contact directory with entity types team player association search filters bulk import export stale record tracking

Design a bounded data formatting utility module that provides consistent date/time parsing and formatting, number formatting with locale support, and string normalization functions. The module should be stateless and expose clear, reusable functions for normalizing and formatting dates, numbers, and strings across different workflows. Include support for common format patterns and locale-aware conversions.
comic series name, issue range, character name, story title, or creator

Create a single-page virtual piano chord playground as a self-contained HTML file. Requirements: 1) Visual piano keyboard spanning 2 octaves (C3-C5) with clickable keys 2) Computer keyboard mapping (QWERTY row plays white keys, number row plays black keys) 3) Audio using Web Audio API oscillator-based piano tone with envelope 4) Chord buttons for: C, Cm, C7, Cm7, Dim, Aug, sus2, sus4 - clicking a chord button highlights its notes on the keyboard and plays the chord 5) Scale selector showing major and natural minor scale degrees overlaid on keys 6) Input field where users can type note names (e.g., 'C4 E4 G4') to play custom combinations 7) Clean, minimal UI with clear key labels. No external dependencies or servers - fully local-first.
User provides: label name, optional metadata (country, year founded, type). System creates label record and allows linking to existing media items or creating new ones.

A user wants to learn animation scripting by studying working examples with inline explanations and having quick access to terminology definitions. They prefer a self-contained tool that works offline and doesn't require account creation or cloud sync.

[User selects difficulty level (1-3), number range (0-10, 0-20, 0-100), operation mode (addition only, subtraction only, mixed)] → [ProblemGenerator renders arithmetic problem in large child-friendly typeface] → [User types answer in input field and presses Enter or clicks Submit] → [InputValidator checks answer against computed result] → [FeedbackRenderer displays immediate visual/audio feedback (checkmark animation for correct, gentle shake and hint for incorrect)] → [SessionTracker increments count, updates session progress bar] → [After N problems, ProgressDisplay shows session summary with score and optional retry prompt]

Design a bounded kiosk launcher module that restricts device access to approved applications and documents only, with admin-protected configuration, password-gated exit options, and automatic session reset for shared device environments.

Design an interactive onboarding demo module that teaches users how to use a software product through clickable examples, guided navigation paths, and contextual help overlays. Include a directory/table of contents for non-linear access, a navigation palette for jumping between sections, interactive demo cards showing scripted UI behaviors, input field exercises where users can enter data and see responses, dynamic computed fields showing live date/time or calculated values, custom menu demonstrations, an audio/visual intro sequence, and persistent help entry points ('?' buttons) on each screen.

Design a wearable companion app module that lets users manage calendar events, alarms, and contacts on their phone and sync them to a paired smartwatch. Include record list views for each data type (calendar, alarms, contacts), edit/create forms, a sync action to push data to the wearable, and status/confirmation feedback showing sync progress and results.

Build a single-file HTML/CSS/JS module that functions as an interactive animation learning sandbox. Include 5-6 simple animation demonstrations (e.g., fade, slide, scale, rotate, easing, bounce) where each example displays the current animation, shows editable parameters (duration, easing, direction, etc.), and provides a live preview that updates when parameters change. Each example should have a brief explanation of the animation principle it demonstrates. The module should work offline and require no server dependencies. Include a simple gallery view to browse examples and a focused detail view for experimenting with each animation.

User needs to view, add, edit, or search personal/business contacts with notes and categories, then perform actions like one-click call via VoIP, print address labels for Avery templates, or export selected contacts to vCard/CSV

Design a bounded local-first personal command hub module that accepts typed natural-language input to create tasks, lookup contacts, and trigger common organizer actions. The module should provide a single home surface showing active tasks and recent contacts, parse natural language like "remind me tomorrow to call mom" into structured task data, integrate with local contacts, and route commands to appropriate handlers. No cloud sync, no broad suite features, no nostalgia—focus purely on personal information management with natural language efficiency.

Build a self-contained Note Zoo module — a musical learning activity for preschool children. Display 8 animal characters in a colorful scene, each representing a note in the C major octave (C4 to C5). When a child taps/clicks an animal, play the corresponding piano note and display the note name (e.g., 'D') in large, friendly text. Keep it simple: no accounts, no progress saving, no internet required. Use large touch targets, clear illustrations, and immediate audio feedback. The animals should suggest the pitch height visually (smaller animals = higher pitch or vice versa) to reinforce the concept that higher pitch = higher note.

You are building a bounded temperature conversion module. Create a compact HTML/CSS/JS component that accepts a numeric temperature value and a source unit (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin, or Rankine), then displays computed equivalents for all remaining units in a scannable results table. Include input validation for physically meaningful temperature ranges (e.g., no negative Kelvin values). Use local-first design—no external API calls required.

Design a bounded local-first module for bootstrapping new software projects and running development helper scripts. The module should: (1) contain copyable starter templates for initializing common project types, (2) provide callable script helpers for recurring development tasks, (3) expose a CLI interface for template generation and script execution, (4) be embeddable within a developer's project without external service dependencies. Use Hygen as the underlying generator engine. Include template definitions for at least one project type and demonstrate how a developer invokes a helper script via the CLI.

Implement an interactive map module. It loads a background map image (or SVG) and renders clickable hotspot markers at defined coordinates. Clicking a hotspot displays a detail panel with the location name and description. Include pan/zoom controls and a simple index list for quick navigation. The location data is a local JSON array embedded in the module, with no external API dependencies. The module should work offline once loaded, and be embeddable in a web page as a self-contained component.

Design a bounded local-first module for batch find-and-replace operations across text files. Include search input, replace input, regex support, scope controls to select multiple files or records, a preview pane showing matches in context, confirm/apply actions, and an undo mechanism. The module should be a standalone utility or embeddable panel, not a full text editor or CMS.

Build a compact floating command palette module that persists above other workspace windows. Include a row of icon buttons that trigger navigation actions (first, previous, next, last, recent) and authoring actions (open, close, push, pop, launch). Implement modifier-key detection (Alt, Ctrl, Shift) to expand each button's action set beyond the visible icons—single click performs default action, modifier+click performs variant. Include a recent-items submenu, window/view selector dropdown, safe-mode toggle that disables script handlers and transition effects for debugging, and inline tooltips. Design for mouse-first interaction with keyboard shortcuts as secondary. The palette should be draggable and remember its position.

>>> You are building a local-first Dua (Supplication) Reader module. Your bounded job: - Provide a focused, serene reading experience for curated Islamic supplications (duas) - Let users browse duas by category/occasion or search - Allow bookmarking meaningful duas - Keep it simple, beautiful, and offline-capable Constraints: - ONE bounded module: just the dua reading experience (not Quran, not full prayer suite) - Local-first: data lives on device - No account required, no sync, no cloud features - Clean, distraction-free reading typography - Categories should cover: morning/evening, forgiveness, health, guidance, gratitude, protection, praise, etc. Design the module structure, data model for duas, and core UI flows.

You are building a bounded menu-integration module for a desktop application shell. Your module needs to: (1) Accept custom About text and optional Help menu label as parameters at initialization, (2) Insert branded entries into the host application's top-level menu (Apple menu on macOS), (3) Handle menu item clicks to show an About modal and navigate to Help, (4) Store the original menu state before modification, (5) Provide a dispose/restore method that returns the menu to its original state—call this in your container's unload or close handler. Write clean, parameter-driven code that follows the pattern: new AppMenuIntegrator(aboutText, helpLabel?), .install(), .onAction(callback), .dispose().

Implement a bounded JavaScript module providing randomized visual effects for interactive UI experiences. The module should: 1) Export an effects library with at least 8-10 distinct effect types (scale, rotate, translate, opacity, blur, color-shift, shake, bounce, pulse, wiggle) each accepting randomized parameter ranges; 2) Provide a randomSelector that can pick effects uniformly or with weights; 3) Accept trigger configuration for click, hover, scroll, or interval-based activation; 4) Allow option overrides for duration, easing, delay, and intensity; 5) Use anime.js for the underlying animation and export a simple API: applyEffect(element, effectType, options), applyRandomEffect(element, options), setTriggers(elements, triggerConfig). No server-side code required - this is a client-side library.

Design and implement a bounded local-first audio recording input control utility module that exposes device enumeration, input source selection, gain level control, and error-safe capture operations with cross-platform support
design a selection manager module for a list/detail view that allows users to mark individual records for batch operations and clear all selections with one action
Design a minimal local command dispatcher module that exposes an HTTP endpoint to receive and execute commands against local applications or trigger actions on other machines in your network.

A bounded window picker module enabling rapid window switching, especially for multiple windows of the same application. Features: enumerate all visible windows with titles and app icons, floating selection panel, keyboard navigation (up/down/enter), single-action focus, global hotkey invocation, escape to dismiss. Constrain to window-switching only - no repositioning, resizing, or workspace management.

Write a JavaScript module that renders 1-3 animated character sprites on an HTML canvas. Each character cycles through a sprite sheet animation loop (idle, walk). Characters move randomly within canvas bounds using procedural motion - random direction changes at intervals, boundary collision, smooth interpolation. The module exports a Scene class with start(), stop(), setCharacterCount(n), and updateConfig() methods. Use requestAnimationFrame for the game loop.

Design and implement a bounded JavaScript/TypeScript module for formatting numbers as currency, signed values, or decimal numbers. Use Intl.NumberFormat for locale-aware output. Support parsing input strings to numeric values. Keep it a single-purpose utility—do not expand into charting, calculations, or full accounting. Export clean, testable functions.

Build a reusable progress dialog component with: determinate mode (0-100 value), indeterminate mode, customizable label text, optional icon, cancel button with callback, and theming support. It should handle multiple concurrent progress windows and integrate cleanly with async operations.

Design and implement a local-first opponent scouting module for youth baseball coaches. The module should allow coaches to: 1) quickly add opponent players with name and typical batting position, 2) log each observed at-bat by tapping the hit location on an interactive baseball field diagram and selecting the outcome (single, double, triple, home run, fly out, ground out, line drive, pop out, strikeout, walk, hit by pitch), 3) automatically aggregate hit locations per player into a visual spray chart or heat map, 4) generate a one-page pregame report showing each opponent hitter's tendencies (pull/opposite/center, ground ball % vs fly ball %, hot zones), 5) store all data locally in the browser or on-device. The interface should be thumb-friendly for use on the sideline or bleacher, require no account or login, and work fully offline once loaded.

Define a nonstandard experimental design by specifying factors, their levels, the randomization structure (CRD, RCBD, split-plot, Latin square, etc.), and whether each factor is fixed or random. The tool then outputs the expected mean squares for each source in the ANOVA.
local icon file or clipboard bitmap

Design a bounded Protocol Console module that enables developers to connect to any remote text-based server, view live bidirectional transcript of the exact bytes sent and received (in ASCII and hex), manually compose and send line-by-line commands, and log the full session for analysis. The module should support both TCP and UDP connections, allow saving/loading connection profiles, and export session transcripts. Target users are developers and system administrators debugging network services, testing protocol implementations, or performing network diagnostics.

Create a classroom drawing module for K-12 students. Features: canvas workspace with brush, color picker, eraser tools; save drawing to local portfolio; portfolio gallery view showing all saved works; optional print/export. Local-first storage (no cloud required), privacy-appropriate for schools. Bounded to drawing and portfolio viewing only - not a full creative suite.
any image file (screenshot, photo, design export)

Create a personal media catalog module that allows users to: 1) Add physical media items with title, format type, condition, storage location, and custom tags. 2) Search and filter the collection by any field. 3) Generate and print labels using Avery-compatible templates (CD/DVD spine labels, media box labels, etc.). 4) Export collection data to CSV for backup. The app should be local-first with no cloud dependency.

You are building MelodyClip Workbench, a focused local-first module for editing and organizing short MIDI melody clips. The user wants to: (1) create or load short melodic sequences, (2) transform them (transpose key, change octave, adjust duration, modify velocity, swap instrument), (3) assemble clips into a reusable playlist/library, (4) export or drag-drop clips into other projects or VST plugins. Design the module with a clip editor view, transform controls, playlist panel, and project bridge. Keep it bounded - this is not a full DAW but a clip-specific editor and organizer. Prioritize local storage, simple file formats (MIDI, JSON clip metadata), and direct VST/DAW integration over cloud features.

Create a bounded educational module for kids ages 6-12 that lets them browse facts about planets and the sun. The module should have: 1) A landing view showing all planets plus the sun as tappable cards or list items; 2) Individual planet detail views with consistent attributes (size, distance from sun, weather/atmosphere, number of moons, fun fact); 3) A simple comparison mode where 2-3 planets can be shown side-by-side with their attributes aligned. The UI should be colorful and child-friendly with large touch targets. Use local storage so it works offline. No user accounts, no external links, no multimedia beyond static images/icons.

Build a bounded early numeracy learning module with two core activities: digit tracing with animated demonstrations showing proper number formation (e.g., animal characters demonstrating strokes), and object counting with tap interactions that provide audio feedback as children count. Include progress tracking, a limited number set (1-5), and an age-appropriate visual theme. The module should work offline, store data locally, and require no internet connection after initial installation.

Create a bounded software module for looking up chemical hazard classifications and workplace exposure limits. The module accepts a chemical identifier (name, CAS number, or molecular formula) and returns: (1) hazard categories (GHS/OSHA: carcinogen, teratogen, mutagen, toxic, irritant, corrosive, oxidizer, lachrymator); (2) numeric exposure limits (OSHA PEL, TLV, IDLH); (3) basic PPE guidance. Prioritize static local data for environments with intermittent or no internet access. Use a clean tabular layout for exposure data and icon-driven labels for hazard categories. Include a compact export function for printing or PDF generation of a single chemical record. The UI should be utilitarian and high-contrast for industrial environments.

Create a bounded developer utility that renders visible outlines around UI components (buttons, fields, inputs) in a design-time preview environment. The tool should allow toggling outlines on/off, support configurable outline colors/styles, and optionally scope rendering to specific component types or zones. It must not affect runtime/production code or performance.

A list of time/duration entries as text (return or comma delimited), each entry may include an optional label prefix, supports formats: bare integers (minutes), HH:MM, duration strings like '1h30m', negative values for credits

Create a reusable DynamicInputModal component that accepts a configuration object defining: (1) 1-3 text input fields with labels and validation rules, (2) optional radio group with 1-4 options, (3) 1-4 action buttons with distinct handlers. The component should: render the modal with proper focus trap, collect all field values into a result object, track the selected radio option, identify which button was clicked, and return { fields: {...}, radioSelection: string|null, triggeredAction: string } on completion. Include keyboard navigation, escape-to-close, and proper ARIA attributes for accessibility.

Build a bounded local-first desktop application for studying 'All's Well That Ends Well' by William Shakespeare. Requirements: display the full play text segmented by act/scene; render inline scholarly annotations that appear on user interaction (not cluttering the reading view); provide a navigation panel to jump between scenes; include a personal notes margin for the reader's own annotations; persist reading position and personal notes locally; allow searching across both the primary text and annotations; keep the scope strictly limited to this single work - do not build a generic annotation platform.

I need to generate disabled-state styling and visual assets for a design system. The module should accept component type and base styling, apply a disabled treatment (opacity, grayscale, overlay), and output CSS rules, design tokens, and optionally SVG/PNG assets. Include configuration for opacity levels, overlay colors, and preview comparison. This is a bounded utility, not a full design system.

Create a single-page interactive visualizer that teaches five physics concepts: force, work, rate, resistance, and energy. Include a main menu that lets users select which concept to explore. For each concept, provide: (1) an HTML5 Canvas animation that visually demonstrates the principle with moving objects, (2) one or more sliders to adjust key variables, (3) brief explanatory text (2-3 sentences), and (4) real-time metrics display showing relevant measurements. The design should feel like a modern educational tool—clean, minimal, with subtle animations and a soft color palette. Use vanilla JavaScript and CSS with no external dependencies. Each demo should run smoothly at 60fps. Include a 'reset' button for each demo and a 'back to menu' option.
Extract icon assets from application binaries

Design a bounded local-first module that bulk reassigns file associations across a directory tree. Requirements: (1) select root folder, (2) choose file class by extension or UTI pattern, (3) map current association to desired application, (4) preview affected files with confirmation, (5) apply recursively to all subfolders, (6) support custom type mappings. Output: module specification with UI wireframe and data flow.

Design a bounded module that enables in-canvas repositioning and resizing of UI components. Users select an element on a design canvas and drag to move it, or use corner/edge handles to resize. Modifier keys (Shift for aspect ratio lock, Alt for center-origin resize) modify behavior. The module handles selection state, gesture recognition, coordinate transformation, constraint enforcement, and notifies the layout model of changes. No mode switching or property panel required.

Build a bounded local-first module for reading and studying Shakespeare's Henry VI, Part I with TEI-encoded scholarly annotations. The module should display the play text segmented by act/scene, provide linked annotations in a marginal or sidebar panel, include navigation between scenes, support internal cross-references to other passages, and allow basic note-taking tied to specific lines. The interface should be clean, typography-focused, and suitable for sustained scholarly reading.
The user provides: (1) number of grid columns and rows, (2) list of button definitions (label, icon/emoji, action URL or handler), (3) optional visual styling preferences
recipient has a large detailed image file (technical drawing, artwork, scan, diagram) on their local machine and needs to examine it at high magnification without uploading to any service

Create a film script beat board with three act columns (Setup, Confrontation, Resolution), each containing draggable story beat cards. Each card should have editable title and description fields. Include a floating add-beat button, collapsible tips panel with screenplay formatting guidance, and drag-to-reorder functionality within and between columns.
design a local-first desktop app for generating and printing customizable labels for physical media collections

Design a lightweight SSH terminal client module for direct Unix/Linux host access with local-first architecture. The module should handle SSH connection establishment, terminal session management, and encrypted credential storage. Focus on direct connections without requiring VPN or cloud proxy. Include basic SFTP support for file transfers.
Design a localized study module for nervous tissue anatomy and physiology that combines conceptual overview, visual content review, and self-testing with explanatory feedback

Design a bounded module that serves as a symbol browser panel within a development environment. The module lists all registered custom commands, functions, and handlers in the project. Users can search/filter this list and click any entry to jump directly to its source definition. The module must be local-first and integrate with the editor's go-to-definition mechanism.

Create a bounded preschool alphabet learning module with two interaction modes: (1) Guided mode where child clicks large forward/back buttons to advance through alphabet, each screen showing a letter with a matching illustration and optional letter-sound audio, (2) AutoPlay mode where the module automatically cycles through the alphabet with gentle animated transitions, suitable for passive viewing. Target age: toddlers to 5 years. Include simple progress indication. Prioritize large touch targets, bright clear visuals, minimal UI chrome, and immediate responsive feedback.

You are a local-first desktop OS tips and troubleshooting assistant. You provide short, practical, how-to entries organized by topic. Each response should be a brief, actionable tip entry with clear steps. Cover file dialog navigation, system settings customization, Finder/desktop troubleshooting, and icon customization. Do not provide exhaustive system documentation. Keep entries self-contained and scannable. If a task requires deeper troubleshooting, suggest specific next steps rather than broad diagnostics. Prioritize local-first solutions (system preferences, built-in tools) before suggesting third-party tools.
desktop audio visualizer local-first module that renders animated visuals synced to music playback
design a local-first hotspot navigation system where users click scene elements to travel between locations

Design a bounded, local-first module for partitioned long-form text entry where a single large textarea can be split into 2-90 sequential stacked sections. Each section should function as its own mini-editor with navigation to adjacent sections, section numbering, delete capability, and the ability to reorder sections via drag-and-drop. Include a 'merge to unified' mode that recomposes all sections into a single editor for bulk operations. The module should preserve all text and field properties per section and support both cursor-driven section navigation and direct jump-to-section controls.

Single-purpose marble texture generator that creates unique stone/marble backgrounds with adjustable vein patterns, colors, scale, and turbulence. Must include live preview, example gallery, parameter controls, help guidance, and hidden easter egg features. Export to common design formats. Local-first sovereignty, no cloud dependencies.
user needs to browse and preview locally-installed fonts in discrete card records, customize preview text, view font metadata, and select fonts for use in external design tools

User needs to add visual interface assets (icons, micro-animations) to improve project navigation and presentation. User browses/searches, previews animated and static variants, selects assets, exports in chosen format (SVG/PNG/React/HTML/Vue/Svelte), and inserts into project or sends to Figma.
Create a bounded React component module for an animated button microinteraction with tunable physics-based motion, multiple animation variants, and interactive playground documentation
Build a bounded HierarchicalSelect UI module that lets users browse and pick from arbitrarily deep menu structures with dynamic data, icons, and configurable styling.

An embedded storefront creation wizard for solo sellers. The user has a simple product or service to sell. They don't want to configure a merchant account or manage hosting. They need: 1) A guided onboarding flow that captures basic seller info, 2) A simple hosted product/catalog manager, 3) Built-in payment processing that handles the merchant relationship (like Stripe Checkout or Square), 4) A minimal customizable storefront, and 5) Basic order tracking. The system handles all payment infrastructure. Design a bounded module that walks a non-technical user through launching their store in under 10 minutes.

Design a bounded FAQ module for developer documentation that allows creating, organizing, and searching question-answer entries by topics, with support for schema markup and links to related resources
Design a bounded local-first direct messaging module for office environments where employees communicate exclusively over the internal LAN without internet dependency

Build a minimal npm package of reusable canvas drawing utilities for custom app UI graphics. Include common shapes (rect, circle, polygon), path drawing helpers, and fill/stroke configuration. No framework dependencies, just pure functions wrapping Canvas2D API.

Design a bounded module for importing legacy delimited text files (CSV, TSV, byte-delimited) into a modern application. The module should: (1) accept file upload or path selection, (2) auto-detect delimiter, encoding, and column structure, (3) present a field mapping UI to map source columns to destination schema fields, (4) show a preview of transformed records before commit, (5) execute the import with progress indication, (6) report success count, skip count, and actionable error details. Target users are data migrators and system administrators moving data from legacy databases.

Design a bounded personal productivity module that serves as a unified workspace combining word processing, simple drawing tools, structured data tables, chart generation, and basic finance/budget management. The module should function as a hub where all submodules can access and share the same underlying records. Target use case is everyday business or personal work - writing documents, creating simple visuals, maintaining structured records, generating reports with charts, and tracking budgets/finances. The module should be sovereign/local-first, not a cloud suite. Include: document editor with basic formatting, a simple canvas for MacPaint-style drawing, a spreadsheet-like data table for records, a chart/report builder for visualization, and a finance tracker with budget categories and ledger. All components share the same data layer.

You are building a reusable input control module for a modern app framework. The module should provide: 1. A configurable Slider component that supports: - Min/max range with configurable step size - Vertical or horizontal orientation - Continuous or snap-to-point modes - Keyboard and touch accessibility - Event callbacks for value changes 2. A Joystick component that provides: - Directional control with velocity-based output - Spring return to center when released - Configurable deadzone - 2D vector output (x, y magnitude and direction) 3. A unified input event handler that normalizes events across components and provides utilities for value mapping and debouncing. 4. Prebuilt variant components that compose the base controls with common defaults (volume slider, brightness slider, 8-way directional pad for game-like input). Constraints: - Keep this as a focused, bounded module — do not expand into a full design system - Prioritize clean, composable component APIs - Include accessibility support (ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation) - Provide TypeScript types for all public APIs - Document usage with simple, realistic examples Deliver: Component implementations, TypeScript definitions, and usage documentation for each control.

The user is working in a visual editor/IDE/design tool and needs to rapidly modify multiple UI elements' properties, align and space visual components precisely, place assets onto canvas, transform text patterns, and generate reusable code from visual manipulations
User's freehand drawing on HTML canvas
User wants to create a smooth animation path for a UI element (button, icon, character) without hand-coding coordinates

Design a self-contained Character Count Input component that displays real-time character/word remaining capacity for a bounded text field. The component should: attach to input or textarea elements, show remaining character count with configurable max length, provide visual indication as limit approaches (e.g., color change at 80% threshold), include ARIA live region for accessibility, support both character and word count modes, allow customization of warning/critical thresholds, and expose capacity state for external validation. Implementation should be framework-agnostic or provide React/Vue adapters with scoped styling.
